question: A message has a correlation key set, no message ID, and TTL set to 0. It is published while a matching intermediate message catch event subscription is active. What happens?

options.text: It is correlated to the active subscription.
It is buffered until another subscription appears.
A new process instance is started.
An incident is raised because the message ID is missing.

a.text: For an intermediate event with TTL = 0 and a correlation key set, the overview table specifies the message is correlated when a matching subscription is active.

b.text: Buffering only applies when TTL > 0; with TTL = 0 the message is not held for later subscriptions.

c.text: Only message start events create new process instances. An intermediate catch event correlates to an existing subscription.

d.text: Message ID is optional. Only the message name is mandatory; correlation does not require an ID.

explanation: Per the message correlation overview, an intermediate event receives a message with correlation key set, no message ID, and TTL = 0 only if a matching subscription is currently active. Without a live subscription, the message is dropped because there is no buffer window.
